name: Guadalajara - Segovia – circular desde Campisábalos por la senda de los Caracoles
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Campisábalos offer a diverse terrain, characterized by river canyons, varied elevations, and natural features. The region features impressive gorges and cliffs, such as those found in the Caracena River Canyon, alongside areas with dense woodlands and open landscapes. Rivers like the Aguisejo contribute to the natural beauty and provide scenic backdrops for cycling. This varied topography provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Campisábalos The most popular mountain bike trail is Guadalajara - Segovia – circular desde Campisábalos por la senda de los Caracoles, a 26.7 miles (43.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant distances and elevation gains. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Closed due to forest fire loop from Parque Natural Hayedo Tejera Negra, a moderate 13.1 miles (21.1 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through forested areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Galve de Sorbe Castle – Galve de Sorbe loop from Galve de Sorbe, a 29.3 miles (47.1 km) trail leading through scenic landscapes and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Campisábalos is defined by diverse terrain, including river canyons, forested sections, and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Campisábalos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 50 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campisábalos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Campisábalos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Campisábalos feature diverse terrain, including river canyons, varied elevations, and natural features. You'll encounter impressive gorges and cliffs, such as those in the Caracena River Canyon, alongside areas with dense woodlands and open landscapes. Rivers like the Aguisejo also contribute to the scenic beauty.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Campisábalos?
A: While Campisábalos offers a range of trails, the current selection of routes primarily includes moderate and difficult options. There are 10 moderate trails and 5 difficult trails, but no easy trails are specifically listed. Moderate trails are suitable for those with some mountain biking experience.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes available?
A: For those looking for longer rides, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galve de Sorbe Castle – Galve de Sorbe loop from Galve de Sorbe&lt;/a&gt;, which is 47.1 km (29.3 miles) long and typically takes around 4 hours 5 minutes. Another substantial option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006136255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara - Segovia – circular desde Campisábalos por la senda de los Caracoles&lt;/a&gt;, covering 43.0 km (26.7 miles) in about 3 hours 55 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Campisábalos?
A: Yes, many routes around Campisábalos are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006136255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalajara - Segovia – circular desde Campisábalos por la senda de los Caracoles&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Closed due to forest fire loop from Parque Natural Hayedo Tejera Negra&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galve de Sorbe Castle – Galve de Sorbe loop from Galve de Sorbe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Campisábalos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6498473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caracena River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, known for its gorges and cliffs, and discover the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2549872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Stone Arch&lt;/a&gt; within it. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1733842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Aguisejo River&lt;/a&gt; and the rustic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Cerezo&lt;/a&gt; are also notable spots.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the trails?
A: Yes, you can find historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galve de Sorbe Castle&lt;/a&gt; along some routes. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tiermes archaeological site&lt;/a&gt; is another significant point of interest in the broader area.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Campisábalos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campisábalos are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.8 stars from over 5 reviews. Many mountain bikers appreciate the diverse terrain, which includes river canyons, forested sections, and open landscapes, offering varied experiences for different ability levels.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Campisábalos?
A: Yes, there are designated parking areas. For example, you can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6276555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parking lot of the Natural Park Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt;, which serves as a convenient starting point for many routes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer viewpoints or scenic vistas?
A: Absolutely. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacadima – Villacadima Viewpoint loop from Parque Natural Hayedo Tejera Negra&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you past scenic spots, including the Villacadima Viewpoint, offering great panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike rides in Campisábalos?
A: The duration of rides varies depending on the trail and your pace. Moderate routes can range from approximately 2 hours, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Closed due to forest fire loop from Parque Natural Hayedo Tejera Negra&lt;/a&gt; (1 hour 59 minutes), to over 4 hours for longer moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galve de Sorbe Castle – Galve de Sorbe loop from Galve de Sorbe&lt;/a&gt; (4 hours 5 minutes). Difficult routes also fall within this range, depending on their length and elevation gain.

Q: Can I find trails that follow rivers or water features?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Aguisejo River – Park entrance loop from La Senda de los Caracoles&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, allowing you to cycle near the Aguisejo River. The region's natural beauty is often enhanced by rivers like the Aguisejo, providing scenic backdrops for your ride.
tips: Abandoned settlement that is located in a fairly remote and picturesque place, which is reached by a cool track with a good surface.

waypoints.name: Abandoned Village of Sotillo de Caracena
